ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-14-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steve Bovarnick for Paul Balas.

Mr. Bovarnick stated that he filed a consent from the Public Administrator and a detailed declaration on June 23, 2014. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Stewart. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-23-2014 Continued To Jun-30-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-16-2014 Continued To Jun-23-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Louie for Cheryl Balas (Intestate Heir). James Allen for Paul Balas (Administrator).

The Court expressed concerns with the following: 1) the amount of money spent on securing the audio equipment, 2) travel expenses & cleaning including the fact that the administrator's wife was on some of the trips, 3) the size of the reserve requested, 4) that the 2012 taxes have not been paid, and 5) the distributions valued at the dates of distribution. Mr. Allen stated that there was a huge collection of audio equipment, that it took time to organize it, that Mr. Balas had expected the collection to be worth more money, and that it was difficult to find someone to appraise the collection. The Court stated that it took a long time. Mr. Allen stated that there were problems completing the inventory, that the collection couldn't be divided, that it was a monumental task, that Mr. Balas' wife did make the trip twice to help Mr. Balas, and that Mr. Balas wife helped in cleaning the apartment. The Court stated that there were eight trips by Mr. Balas and that there wasn't a lot in the apartment. Mr. Allen stated that it did take that many times to complete the task(s) and that Mr. Balas, as part of the settlement, waived his extraordinary fees. The Court addressed the "settlement" stating that there is an agreement of two parties but that there remains one party that needs protection. The Court inquired whether Mr. Allen would like more time to get all parties to sign off on the settlement. Mr. Allen addressed the closing expenses noting that the amount of $25,000.00 was kind of high and that he'd check with the accountant. The Court continued the matter June 23, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Allen to obtain the consent or agreement of the Conservator.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing Of Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-05-2013 Continued To Aug-05-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Allen for Paul Balas (Petitioner/Administrator).

Mr. Allen explained that there is a medical issue with Rhoda Nadel (one of the Heirs) located in San Diego and that the San Diego Public Conservator will petition for an LPS Conservatorship. The Court explained to Counsel that a declaration is not sufficient, and that he needs to file either a Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval of Status Report. Status on filing of a Petition for Final Distribution was continued to August 5, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for compliance with Probate Code 12200.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
